name: Etruscan

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Etruscan (etru1241).

notes: Skeleton. Extinct, non-Indo-European (Tyrsenian) language of ancient Italy written in the Old Italic (Etruscan) alphabet. Grapheme map assigns each letter its conventional reconstructed value: four vowels a e i u (o is written but was not distinct from u), the aspirated stops θ [tʰ], φ [pʰ], χ [kʰ], the three redundant velar letters c/k/q all [k], z [ts], two sibilants s and ś [ʃ], and f written with the 8-shaped letter. Etruscan had essentially no voiced stops, so b and d occur only in loans. Reconstructed values carry inherent uncertainty; vowel length, stress and syncope are not recoverable from the spelling and are left unencoded, so a substantial PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Etruscan (etru1241)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
